summary: Current transition snapshot after Discover + Stats doctrine locks, Plot feed/notification/calendar clarification, and lean-context workflow rules. Active branch is feat/ux-founder-locks-and-harness at commit 1245891; worktree is still dirty from unrelated non-doc changes.
locks: Home is the left destination, Discover is the right destination, and Plot lives inside Home.
Discover is player-anchored and inherits the active listening scope rather than creating a parallel context system.
Discover recommendations are explicit user-to-signal relationships rendered through the avatar + fixed balloon grammar.
Discover Popular Singles uses the MVP lenses Most Added, Supported Now, and Recent Rises.
Popular Now remains owned by RaDIYo or broadcast momentum logic and is not a Discover lens.
Statistics are a shared descriptive contract for Plot, Discover, and future analytics, separate from the deferred Activity Points or Activity Score system.
Plot feed carries explicit scene actions and followed-source updates.
The profile-strip notification icon is a separate system or state-change notice surface, not a second activity feed.
Calendar auto-population remains part of MVP organizational behavior.
Top 40 or billboard-style lists are deferred from MVP until population justifies them.
Lean-context operation is now a documented workflow rule: salvage early, avoid transcript dumps, and separate doctrine threads from implementation threads.
open_decisions: Exact notification taxonomy beyond the locked feed-vs-notification split.
Exact map visualization treatment by structural scope.
Activity Points or Activity Score formula and decay semantics.
Top 40 or billboard tie-break semantics if those surfaces are intentionally reintroduced later.
drift_corrections: Do not treat Plot as a peer destination to Home.
Do not treat recommendations as freeform user-written text.
Do not reuse RaDIYo's Popular Now momentum semantics inside Discover.
Do not move followed-source updates into the notification icon by default.
Do not treat Search Party or sect private channels as MVP feed behavior.
Do not treat Top 40 or billboard lists as current MVP requirements.
next_actions: Start a clean implementation-facing session rather than continuing ontology work in a dense doctrine thread.
Translate Discover and stats locks into an apps/web implementation plan.
Reconcile any remaining prompt packs or docs that still imply stale Discover or Top 40 behavior.
If needed, add a dedicated founder lock for notification classes.
Avoid auditing or implementing against the current mixed unrelated worktree without explicit ownership.
